rulings.Case Title: ALEKSANDR RABINOVICH VS. FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY DBA FIRST
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 9, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANTS DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, TRUSTEE FOR, WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. DBA WELLS FARGO HOME MOTION TO STRIKE FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT. Motion to strike off calendar as moot in view of court's ruling on demurrer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ALEKSANDR RABINOVICH VS. FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY DBA FIRST
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 9, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANTS DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, TRUSTEE FOR, WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. DBA WELLS FARGO HOME DEMURRER TO FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend for failure to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action. CCP section 430.10(e). The complaint is comprised mostly of legal conclusions. Allegations constituting legal conclusions are not admitted on a demurrer as they are not facts. Kenneth Mebane vs. Superior Court (1992) 10 Cal.App.4th 276, 291. There is no reasonable probability that plaintiff can cure these defects (see Aubry vs. Tri-City Hospital District (1992) 2 Cal.4th 962) in light of plaintiff's failure to do so on prior court orders, such as the 11-25-09 order sustaining Deutsche Bank's demurrer with leave to amend. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PACIFIC CAPITAL BANK N.A., DBA FIRST NATIONAL VS. THE SHEMANO GROUP, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Continuance Of Hearing Of Demurrers To Third Amended Cross Complaint Of The Shemano Group
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 9, 2010, line 3, PLAINTIFF PACIFIC CAPITAL BANK N.A., DBA FIRST NATIONAL Notice Of Continuance Of Hearing Of Demurrers To Third Amended Cross Complaint Of The Shemano Group. (Duplicate entry.)

rulings.Case Title: PACIFIC CAPITAL BANK N.A., DBA FIRST NATIONAL VS. THE SHEMANO GROUP, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 9, 2010, line 3, CROSS DEFENDANTS PACIFIC CAPITAL BANK N.A., DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. Cross-complainant fails to plead facts sufficient to establish that the relationships between the parties gives raise to a duty from cross-defendant to cross-complainant. No authority supports plaintiff's contention that the bank owed cross-complainant, who was not a recipient of any loan proceeds, a duty to exercise reasonable care in the underwriting of the loan to Del Biaggio. No facts are plead to suggest it would have been foreseeable to the bank that cross-complainant would be defrauded by its own employee (Cacchione) thereby exposing cross-complainant to liability to third parties. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GLORIA TRAN VS. BAYVIEW CAPITAL, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On Pleadings As To Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 9, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANTS RACHEL GISHKIN, KAREN SARID, SHELLA CERVANTES, MARK CERVANTES, CHI-LEUNG CHENG, ALICE CHANG, THEODORE GRADMAN Motion for Judgment on Pleadings as to Third Amended Complaint. Motion denied without prejudice. Motion is properly made. Furthermore, a right to an accounting is derivative; it must be based on other claims." JAMS vs. California State Lottery Commission (1998) 68 Cal.App. 4th 833-834, citing Union Bank vs. Superior Court (1995) 31 Cal.App. 4th 573, 593-594. Here, lender defendants are named in the fifth cause of action, Count III and the sixth cause of action for accounting. Motion does not address the fifth cause of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ANA VEGA VS. OUTBACK STEAKHOUSE OF FLORIDA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Quashing Service Of Summons Due To Lack Of Jurisdiction; Or In The Alternative Request For Dismissal Or Stay Of Action For Inconvient Forum
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 9, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANT OUTBACK STEAKHOUSE OF FLORIDA, OSI RESTAURANT PARTNERS Motion for Order Quashing Service of Summons Due to Lack of Jurisdiction; or in the Alternative Request For Dismissal or Stay of Action for Inconvient Forum. Motion to quash granted. No supplemental briefing filed by February 25th and March 4th. Plaintiff fails to show that defendants have sufficient minimum contacts with California to justify jurisdiction. (See DVI, Inc. vs. Superior Court (2002) 104 Cal.App. 4th 1080) No substantial, continuous and systematic contacts to justify general jurisdiction. Hesse vs. Best Western International, Inc. (1995) 32 Cal.App. 4th 404 distinquishable in that this case presents less evidence of defendants' overall contacts with the state to support jurisdiction. No specific jurisdiction where this complaint arises out of an accident taking place in Nevada, and not from defendant's California - related activities. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JITENDRA VORA VS. ONE WEST BANK, FSB (AS SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause To Defendants Re: Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 9, 2010, line 9, Plaintiff's Order To Show Cause To Defendants Re: Preliminary Injunction. Motion is denied. Although plaintiff demonstrates an inadequate remedy at law in that the property is unique, and demonstrates imminent and irreparable harm because the property is his family's home, he fails to present evidence that shows a probability of success on the merits. Defendant presents evidece that it purchased plaintiff's loan and deed of trust in December 2009, it has an interest in the property, and the authority to foreclose. There, quiet title claim unlikely to succeed. Wrongful foreclosure unlikely to succeed because no foreclosure has occurred. Defendant presents evidence that FFB did comply with 2923.5 requirements to contact the borrower and discuss options regarding foreclosure. No evidence presented regarding fraud, misrepresentation or concealment. No evidence or facts presented to establish existence of a fiduciary relationship between defendant and plaintiff, something beyond a borrower-lender relationship. No evidence supports the unfair business practices cause of action. =(302/CWW)
